subject: [PATCH 2/4] mm: compaction: support non-movable compaction for pageblock requests

reviews.author: Vlastimil (SUSE)
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch does not consider the case of non-movable pages being allocated in movable blocks, and suggested considering this scenario as well.
sentiment_signals: requested consideration for additional edge case
reviews.raw_body:
But also vice versa, non-movable pages into movable blocks? (without
defrag_mode?).

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges a concern about non-movable compaction requests and explains the current behavior, stating that movable requests are allowed to look for movables in unmovable blocks once they become sync.
reviews.raw_body:
Uhm but those aren't compactable anymore then, right?

There is a flipside, but it isn't quite symmetrical. Movable requests
are allowed to look for movables in unmovable blocks once they become
sync (high-effort, low-result). Non-movable requests are finally
allowed to empty movable blocks once they become sync; they *start*
with the high-effort, low-result mode to avoid block contamination but
are allowed to escalate when that doesn't produce results.

I'll try to work that second part into the comment as well.

2026-07-01.patch_summary: This patch extends the support for compaction of pageblock requests to include non-movable blocks, which was previously limited to movable blocks. This change is a follow-up to earlier patches that restricted asynchronous compaction to prevent block pollution and improve performance. The patch aims to allow allocation fallbacks to compact non-movable pages, reducing the effort required for compaction while still achieving the desired results.
